title: Eduversal AI Competency Framework v1.0 — Part 3: Institutional AI Maturity Framework
sourceDocument: AI Competency Framework — For Eduversal Partner Schools (v1.0 PDF)
sourcePart: Part Three: Institutional AI Maturity Framework

_meta.purpose: Verbatim canonical content for Part 3 of the Eduversal AI Competency Framework v1.0. Source of truth for institutional AI maturity assessment. Chip refIds follow pattern institutional.{domain}.{level} — e.g. institutional.strategy_leadership.level3.
_meta.usage: Academic Directorate uses this during school appraisal visits. Schools self-assess against these descriptors annually.

verbatim: The Institutional AI Maturity Framework enables assessment of school-level AI integration. It provides common criteria for evaluating progress across the Eduversal network, informing school improvement planning and enabling benchmarking.

maturityLevels._overview: School AI maturity is assessed across five levels.

levels.name: Emerging
levels.summary: The school is beginning to engage with AI integration. Awareness is limited, and systematic approaches are not yet in place.

levels.name: Developing
levels.summary: The school has initiated AI integration with basic policies and some staff engagement. Implementation is inconsistent and capabilities are still building.

levels.name: Established
levels.summary: The school has implemented coherent AI integration with clear policies, majority staff engagement, and consistent practices. AI enhances teaching and learning across the school.

levels.name: Advanced
levels.summary: The school demonstrates sophisticated AI integration with strong impact on teaching, learning, and operations. The school contributes to network learning and development.

levels.name: Leading
levels.summary: The school exemplifies outstanding AI integration, serving as a model for the network and contributing to broader educational practice.

assessmentDomains._overview: Institutional AI maturity is assessed across six domains.

domains.title: Strategy and Leadership
domains.scope: The quality of strategic planning, leadership commitment, and governance related to AI integration.

domains.title: Policy and Compliance
domains.scope: The comprehensiveness and implementation of AI policies, including data privacy, academic integrity, and responsible use.

domains.title: Staff Capability
domains.scope: The extent and depth of staff AI competency and the quality of professional development.

domains.title: Teaching and Learning
domains.scope: The quality and consistency of AI integration in classroom practice and curriculum.

domains.title: Student Outcomes
domains.scope: Student AI literacy development and the impact of AI integration on learning outcomes.

domains.title: Infrastructure and Resources
domains.scope: The adequacy of technical infrastructure, tools, and resources to support AI integration.

level1.name: Emerging
level1.verbatim: School leadership has limited awareness of AI integration opportunities and implications. No formal AI strategy exists, and AI initiatives are ad hoc if present at all. AI is not addressed in school improvement planning. Leadership has not communicated clear expectations regarding AI use.

level2.name: Developing
level2.verbatim: School leadership recognises the importance of AI integration and has begun planning. An initial AI integration plan exists but may be incomplete or not fully implemented. AI is mentioned in school improvement documents but not systematically addressed. Leadership has communicated basic expectations but engagement is inconsistent.

level3.name: Established
level3.verbatim: School has a clear AI integration strategy aligned with improvement priorities and Cambridge standards. Leadership actively champions AI integration and models positive engagement. AI integration is systematically addressed in school improvement planning with specific goals and measures. Leadership regularly communicates expectations and celebrates progress.

level4.name: Advanced
level4.verbatim: AI strategy is comprehensive, well-implemented, and clearly impacting school effectiveness. Leadership demonstrates sophisticated understanding and provides strategic direction. AI integration is embedded in school culture and continuous improvement processes. Leadership engages with network and external developments to inform school strategy.

level5.name: Leading
level5.verbatim: School is recognised across the network for outstanding AI strategy and leadership. Leadership contributes to network-level strategy and supports other schools. AI integration is exemplary and continuously evolving based on evidence and emerging practice. School serves as a model and resource for others.

level1.name: Emerging
level1.verbatim: Limited or no formal AI policies exist. Staff are uncertain about expectations and appropriate practice. Data privacy practices related to AI are ad hoc. Academic integrity policies do not address AI.

level2.name: Developing
level2.verbatim: Basic AI policies exist but may be incomplete or inconsistently implemented. Staff awareness of policies is uneven. Data privacy requirements are communicated but compliance is not systematically monitored. Academic integrity policies mention AI but guidance is limited.

level3.name: Established
level3.verbatim: Comprehensive AI policies address staff use, student use, data privacy, and academic integrity. Policies are clearly communicated and consistently implemented. Data privacy compliance is monitored and maintained. Academic integrity policies provide clear guidance on AI and are enforced consistently.

level4.name: Advanced
level4.verbatim: Policies are comprehensive, well-implemented, and regularly reviewed. Staff and students demonstrate strong understanding and consistent adherence. Data governance is robust with proactive monitoring. Academic integrity approach is sophisticated and maintains rigour while enabling beneficial AI use.

level5.name: Leading
level5.verbatim: Policy framework is exemplary and serves as a model for the network. Policies evolve proactively based on emerging developments. Data governance practices are outstanding. Academic integrity approach successfully balances innovation and rigour, informing network practice.

level1.name: Emerging
level1.verbatim: Few staff have AI knowledge or skills beyond basic awareness. No systematic professional development is provided. Staff AI use is minimal and ad hoc. Significant capability gaps exist with no clear plan to address them.

level2.name: Developing
level2.verbatim: Some staff have developed AI skills, but capability is unevenly distributed. Initial professional development has been provided but coverage is incomplete. A minority of staff use AI regularly for professional tasks. Capability development plan exists but implementation is early stage.

level3.name: Established
level3.verbatim: Majority of staff have achieved Foundation level competency. Systematic professional development programme is implemented. Most staff use AI regularly and effectively for appropriate tasks. Clear progression pathways exist and staff are developing toward higher levels.

level4.name: Advanced
level4.verbatim: Strong majority of staff demonstrate Practitioner level competency. Professional development is comprehensive and differentiated. Staff AI use consistently enhances professional effectiveness. Teacher leaders actively support colleagues and contribute to school development.

level5.name: Leading
level5.verbatim: Staff capability is outstanding across the school. Professional development is exemplary and continuously improving. Staff practice serves as a model for the network. Teacher leaders contribute to network professional development and broader professional learning.

level1.name: Emerging
level1.verbatim: AI integration in teaching is minimal or absent. Where present, AI use is ad hoc without clear pedagogical purpose. No systematic approach to developing student AI literacy. AI-enhanced learning experiences are rare.

level2.name: Developing
level2.verbatim: Some teachers integrate AI into their practice with positive effect. AI integration is inconsistent across the school. Initial attention to student AI literacy but not systematic. Some evidence of AI enhancing learning but impact is limited.

level3.name: Established
level3.verbatim: AI integration is consistent across departments and year groups. Clear pedagogical rationale guides AI use in teaching. Student AI literacy curriculum is implemented systematically. Evidence that AI integration enhances learning outcomes.

level4.name: Advanced
level4.verbatim: AI integration is sophisticated and consistently impactful. Teachers use AI strategically to address specific learning needs. Student AI literacy development is strong across year groups. Clear evidence of positive impact on student learning and outcomes.

level5.name: Leading
level5.verbatim: Teaching practice exemplifies outstanding AI integration. Innovative approaches are developed and shared across network. Student AI literacy is excellent and prepares them well for future. School contributes to broader understanding of effective AI-enhanced pedagogy.

level1.name: Emerging
level1.verbatim: Student AI literacy is not systematically developed or assessed. Students have limited understanding of AI. Responsible AI use among students is inconsistent. No evidence of AI integration improving student outcomes.

level2.name: Developing
level2.verbatim: Initial attention to student AI literacy with some positive results. Students demonstrate basic AI awareness. Student responsible use is developing but inconsistent. Some evidence of positive impact but limited.

level3.name: Established
level3.verbatim: Students demonstrate age-appropriate AI literacy across competency strands. Responsible AI use is established as norm. Student outcomes benefit from AI-enhanced teaching and learning. Students are prepared for AI-integrated further study and work.

level4.name: Advanced
level4.verbatim: Students demonstrate strong AI literacy exceeding age-group expectations. Sophisticated responsible use is evident. Clear positive impact on student outcomes from AI integration. Students demonstrate readiness for AI-transformed future.

level5.name: Leading
level5.verbatim: Student AI literacy is exemplary and serves as network model. Students demonstrate outstanding responsible use and critical thinking. Strong evidence of AI integration enhancing outcomes across measures. Students are exceptionally well-prepared for future.

level1.name: Emerging
level1.verbatim: Technical infrastructure is insufficient for effective AI integration. Few or no approved AI tools are available. Budget allocation for AI is minimal or absent. Technical support for AI integration is limited.

level2.name: Developing
level2.verbatim: Basic infrastructure supports initial AI integration. Some approved tools are available but access may be uneven. Budget allocation is limited but growing. Technical support is available but capacity is stretched.

level3.name: Established
level3.verbatim: Infrastructure reliably supports AI integration across the school. Appropriate approved tools are available and accessible. Adequate budget allocation for AI tools and development. Technical support meets school needs.

level4.name: Advanced
level4.verbatim: Infrastructure is robust and enables sophisticated AI integration. Comprehensive tool ecosystem supports diverse needs. Strong budget allocation reflects priority of AI integration. Technical support is responsive and proactive.

level5.name: Leading
level5.verbatim: Infrastructure is outstanding and serves as network model. Tool ecosystem is exemplary and continuously evaluated. Resource allocation enables innovation and leadership. Technical capacity supports school's role as network resource.

verbatim: Schools should conduct annual self-assessment against this framework, typically at the start of the academic year. Self-assessment involves gathering evidence across all domains, rating current level for each domain based on criteria, identifying strengths and areas for improvement, and setting targets for the coming year.
Self-assessment should involve leadership team, department heads, and representative teachers to ensure comprehensive perspective.

Eduversal school appraisal visits will include assessment of AI integration using this framework. Appraisal teams will review self-assessment evidence, observe classroom practice, interview staff and students, and validate or adjust school self-assessment ratings.
Appraisal findings will inform school improvement recommendations and network reporting.

Network-level reporting will enable schools to understand their position relative to network expectations and peer schools. Annual network AI integration report will summarise progress and identify priorities.

_indexing.competencyBlockMatrix: 6 domains × 5 maturity levels = 30 maturity descriptors + 6 domain summaries + 1 overview + 1 maturity_levels overview + 1 domains overview + 1 assessment process = 40 referenceable blocks
